strengths: Production-grade TypeScript and React experience at Optilyz — directly on the core required stack — alongside deep Node.js across multiple employers, covering the role's primary technical requirements end-to-end.

Active AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq (MCP code generators, agentic workflows) directly satisfies the LLM/API integration nice-to-have, a differentiator most junior candidates will lack entirely.

Hands-on AWS CDK (Optilyz) and Kubernetes cluster deployment (GISCollective) cover the cloud/infrastructure nice-to-haves comprehensively, giving the team a candidate who can grow into DevOps-adjacent responsibilities without hand-holding.
concerns: Resume lists Lagos, Nigeria as current location while the role requires high on-site presence — relocation feasibility and timeline need to be confirmed before progressing.
Candidate presents as Staff/Senior level (15+ years, current Staff Engineer title); a Junior/Mid-level scope may under-utilise them and risk early attrition once the novelty of the domain wears off.

No explicit PostgreSQL mention — SQL and MongoDB appear but the preferred relational database is unconfirmed; NestJS is similarly absent, requiring a quick validation of how transferable the Node.js/TypeScript backend experience is to the specific framework.

key_factors.factor: LLM/AI integration and cloud depth (MCP, AWS CDK, K8s)

response.justification: Bogdan brings a rare combination of exactly the required stack — TypeScript, React, Node.js at production scale — plus genuine AI/LLM integration experience and cloud infrastructure breadth that most candidates at this level simply won't have; the energy/construction domain is new, but the platform-building pattern (CRM integration, multi-portal products, data-source aggregation) maps closely to his Optilyz and GISCollective work, making the learning curve narrow. The on-site location question is the primary logistical gate to resolve, but on pure technical fit this candidate would hit the ground running and likely accelerate the team's velocity from day one.
